article_title: Bilateral cranioorbital foramina (Hyrtl foramina): crucial anatomical findings in the management of giant olfactory groove meningioma - a case report and literature review
label: Figure 1
caption: Showing the CT imaging with a hypodense fronto-basal lesion with finger-shaped perifocal edema and the T1-weighted MRI image with homogenous contrast-enhancing frontobasal lesion demonstrating mild compression on both lateral ventricles and posterior displacement of the right middle cerebral artery (MCA) without evidence of hydrocephalus.

article_title: Bilateral cranioorbital foramina (Hyrtl foramina): crucial anatomical findings in the management of giant olfactory groove meningioma - a case report and literature review
label: Figure 2
caption: Show the MRA with the bilateral anastomotic branch of the lacrimal artery with the middle meningeal artery.

article_title: Bilateral cranioorbital foramina (Hyrtl foramina): crucial anatomical findings in the management of giant olfactory groove meningioma - a case report and literature review
label: Figure 3
caption: Showing the digital subtraction angiography of the left external carotid artery in four perspectives: posteroanterior view (upper left), lateral view (upper right), posteroanterior view (lower left), and lateral view (lower right). Additionally, the figure shows the middle meningeal artery and the anastomosis, which are marked.

article_title: Bilateral cranioorbital foramina (Hyrtl foramina): crucial anatomical findings in the management of giant olfactory groove meningioma - a case report and literature review
label: Figure 4
caption: Showing the postoperative T1-weighted MRI with no residual tumor tissue.

article_title: Bilateral cranioorbital foramina (Hyrtl foramina): crucial anatomical findings in the management of giant olfactory groove meningioma - a case report and literature review
label: Figure 5
caption: Showing staining with hematoxylin and eosin, low power lens (upper left), high power lens (upper right) and moleculopathological analysis with ki67 (lower left), progesteron receptor (lower right).

article_title: Visual Loss As Primary Manifestation of Olfactory Groove Meningioma
label: Figure 1
caption: CECT Brain (axial view) showing bi-frontal extra-axial space-occupying lesion (red asterisk) measuring 4.8 x 5.0 x 4.8 cm with skull base erosion. There is a presence of perilesional oedema (blue arrow), left midline shift with ipsilateral ventricle compression.CECT: Contrast-enhanced computed tomography

article_title: Visual Loss As Primary Manifestation of Olfactory Groove Meningioma
label: Figure 2
caption: MRI (sagittal view) showing the anterior skull base meningioma (red asterisk) causing mass effect (right more than left), left midline shift, and contralateral early hydrocephalus.

article_title: Visual Loss As Primary Manifestation of Olfactory Groove Meningioma
label: Figure 3
caption: A generally uniform oval nucleus with a central clearing (arrow) and an indistinct cytoplasmic border. In areas, vague whorls of tumour cells are also present (arrow head).

article_title: Visual Loss As Primary Manifestation of Olfactory Groove Meningioma
label: Figure 4
caption: The vascular channels are variable in size with a thickened hyalinised wall. There are several foci of tiny psammoma bodies noted (arrow head)
